title: The US put nuclear waste under a dome on a Pacific island. Now the dome is cracking open.
summary: Nation/World The US put nuclear waste under a dome on a Pacific island. Now the dome is cracking open. Author: Kyle Swenson , The Washington Post Updated: 1 hour ago Published 1 hour ago At 6:45 a.m. on March 1, 1954, the blue sky stretching over the south Pacific Ocean was split open by an enormous red flash. Within seconds, a mushroom cloud towered 4 1/2 miles high over Bikini Atoll in the Marshall Islands. The explosion, the U.S. government's first weaponized hydrogen bomb, was 1,000 times more powerful than the "Little Boy" atomic bomb blast that flattened Hiroshima - and a complete miscalculation. Scientists had underestimated the size of what became known as the "Castle Bravo" test, resulting in an explosion that was two-and-a-half times larger than expected. Radioactive ash dropped …
